cards.back: A relation between a set of inputs (called the domain) and a set of possible outputs (called the range) where each input is related to exactly one output.
A function where each input is related to exactly one output, with no restriction on the number of outputs.
A function where each input is related to exactly one output, and no two distinct inputs are related to the same output.

cards.front: What is the difference between a plain function and a one-to-one function?
cards.back: A plain function has no restriction on the number of outputs, while a one-to-one function ensures that no two distinct inputs have the same output.

cards.front: What is the condition for a function to be one-to-one?
cards.back: A function is one-to-one if and only if it is both injective (injects each input to a unique output) and surjective (covers all outputs).
